A central sector scheme providing income: A central sector scheme providing income support to all landholding farmers families in the country to supplement their financial needs for procuring various inputs related to agriculture and allied activities as well as domestic needs.
Financial benefit of Rs. 6000/- per year: Financial benefit of Rs. 6000/- per year is transferred directly into the bank accounts of farmer families across the country in three equal installments of Rs. 2000/- every four months.
Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogy: Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (PMJAY)
The worlds largest health insurance/ ass: The worlds largest health insurance/ assurance scheme fully financed by the government, providing a health cover of Rs. 5 Lakhs per family per year for secondary and tertiary care hospitalization across public and private empaneled hospitals.
Rs. 5,00,000 health cover per eligible f: Rs. 5,00,000 health cover per eligible family per year. Cashless and paperless access to healthcare services.
Facilitating bank loans between 10 lakh: Facilitating bank loans between 10 lakh and 1 Crore to at least one Scheduled Caste (SC) or Scheduled Tribe (ST) borrower and at least one woman borrower per bank branch for setting up a greenfield enterprise.
Composite loan (inclusive of term loan a: Composite loan (inclusive of term loan and working capital) between Rs 10 lakh and up to Rs 100 lakh for business commencement.
Providing pucca houses to all eligible f: Providing pucca houses to all eligible families/beneficiaries against the slum rehabilitation and affordable housing requirements. Ensures Housing for All in urban areas.
Subsidy ranging from Rs. 1.0 Lakh to Rs. 2.67 Lakh depending on the income category (EWS, LIG, MIG).
A small savings scheme backed by the Gov: A small savings scheme backed by the Government of India targeted exclusively towards the parents of girl children, encouraging them to build a fund for the future education and marriage expenses of their female child.
High compound interest rate (currently a: High compound interest rate (currently approx 8.2%) with full maturity value and tax exemption benefits under Section 80C.
A special State scheme providing financi: A special State scheme providing financial assistance specifically aimed at elder citizens experiencing age-related disabilities or infirmities, enabling them to purchase prescribed physical aids or assisted-living devices.
Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) of Rs. 3,0: Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) of Rs. 3,000 deposited into the senior citizens bank account for purchasing medical/walking aids.
A Maharashtra state initiative intended: A Maharashtra state initiative intended to retain girls in schools, decrease the female foeticide rate, and prevent child marriages by providing staggered financial incentives linked directly to educational milestones.
Total financial grant escalating up to R: Total financial grant escalating up to Rs. 50,000 provided across various educational landmarks if the girl remains unmarried until age 18.
Mahatma Jyotirao Phule Jan Arogya Yojana: Mahatma Jyotirao Phule Jan Arogya Yojana
The flagship health insurance scheme of: The flagship health insurance scheme of Maharashtra government providing comprehensive cashless medical hospitalization for serious diseases requiring hospitalization for specialized surgeries and therapies.
Cashless health coverage of Rs. 5,00,000: Cashless health coverage of Rs. 5,00,000 per family per year for 1356 enumerated procedures.
An independent state-backed top-up schem: An independent state-backed top-up scheme identical and supplementary to PM-KISAN, specifically designed to inject an additional parallel income stream explicitly to landholding farmers in Maharashtra.
An additional Rs. 6000/- per year transf: An additional Rs. 6000/- per year transferred directly to the farmer, thereby doubling their total annual support combining with PM-KISAN to Rs. 12,000/-.
State pension scheme meant to provide a: State pension scheme meant to provide a permanent layer of financial dignity and sustenance to destitute and elderly citizens living below the poverty line in Maharashtra.
Pension of Rs. 1000 to Rs. 1500 per mont: Pension of Rs. 1000 to Rs. 1500 per month deposited physically into the beneficiarys registered bank account.
Financial assistance for girl children f: Financial assistance for girl children from birth to education
